title: Writing - Part 1: Volunteering in the Community
description: Your class has been discussing how volunteering benefits both individuals and communities, and whether it should be encouraged more actively.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the benefits in your notes. You should explain which benefit you consider most significant, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Volunteering helps people develop new skills, build confidence and broaden their perspective.
Community projects run by volunteers provide essential services that governments cannot always fund.
Volunteering experience makes job applications stand out and demonstrates a range of transferable skills.

structure.intro: Introduce volunteering and state the two benefits you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first benefit with supporting examples.
structure.body2: Discuss the second benefit and compare its value to the first.
structure.conclusion: State which benefit you consider most significant and explain your view.

content.modelAnswer:
Volunteering is a powerful force for good, benefiting not only the communities that volunteers serve but also the individuals who give their time. Two key benefits are personal development and the strengthening of community bonds.

Through volunteering, people gain skills and experiences that formal education and employment rarely provide. Whether teaching literacy, supporting elderly residents or working on conservation projects, volunteers develop empathy, leadership and problem-solving abilities. These qualities enrich a person's life and outlook in ways that are difficult to quantify but deeply valuable.

Equally important is volunteering's role in building stronger communities. Many essential services, from food banks to youth clubs, rely heavily on volunteers. These activities bring people together across different backgrounds and generations, creating social connections that reduce isolation and increase community resilience.

In my view, strengthening communities is the more significant benefit. While personal development is undoubtedly valuable, its effects are primarily individual. The positive impact of volunteering on communities is collective, addressing social needs that would otherwise go unmet. A society in which people actively support one another is more cohesive, compassionate and capable of facing shared challenges.
